data.title: Amprius Secures $21 Million Purchase Order for Electric Two and Three-Wheelers
data.description: Amprius Technologies, a prominent provider of silicon anode lithium-ion batteries, headquartered in Fremont, California, disclosed signifying news today. The company, trading under NYSE as AMPX, shared that it had successfully obtained a substantial purchase order worth $21 million. The order was secured from a prominent electric mobility firm situated in China. The procurement specifically pertains to Amprius' SiCore® cylindrical cells, selected for their outstanding performance. These cells are earmarked for a range of light electric vehicles within the client's product line-up, which includes scooters, three-wheelers, and motorcycles. Amprius' SiCore cells are renowned for their exceptional attributes such as high energy density and extended cycle life, making them a favored choice for enhancing electric mobility solutions.

Paychex emphasized its position as an industry leader in HCM services.

data.title: Vitality Acquires Ramp Health to Create New Standard in Integrated Health and Risk Mitigation
data.description: Chicago-based company Vitality has recently acquired Ramp Health as part of their strategy to provide comprehensive healthcare and safety solutions for employers. This acquisition aims to offer integrated services catering to various aspects of employer health risk.

data.title: Tempus Announces Strategic Collaboration with Daiichi Sankyo to Advance AI-Driven Biomarker Discovery and Clinical Differentiation Across an ADC Clinical Program
data.description: In Chicago, Tempus AI, Inc. disclosed a partnership with Daiichi Sankyo (TSE: 4568) to expedite the clinical progression and distinction of an oncology antibody drug conjugate (ADC) program. This alliance will see Daiichi Sankyo utilizing Tempus’ advanced AI technologies and proprietary foundation models, notably PRISM2, to enhance the development of the program. PRISM2 is a cutting-edge multimodal foundation model that merges various data inputs for comprehensive analysis.

data.title: Frontgrade Launches Versal®-Powered SpaceVPX™ Processing and Fiber I/O Platform for Next-Generation Missions
data.description: In Colorado Springs, a company known as Frontgrade™ Technologies revealed new products designed for space and national security missions. The RPMG1-SVPX Reconfigurable Processing Module and FIOM-RPMG1 Fiber Optic Mezzanine Card were introduced to provide scalable processing power and fast connectivity for future missions. The combined use of these products, known as MAMBA (Modular Approach for Mission Processing), aims to enhance mission performance.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures Rise, Oil Prices Tumble On Cautious Hopes For Iran Peace Deal
data.description: The stock market futures increased while oil prices decreased following reports that the U.S. had delivered a peace proposal to Iran. President Trump praised Tehran for displaying rationality in their discussions. The Dow Jones futures saw a rise as optimism grew cautiously regarding a potential peace agreement with Iran.

data.title: Cresilon Named One of the World's 50 Most Innovative Companies by Fast Company
data.description: Cresilon, a leading company in the medical devices sector, has secured the top spot in this year's Fast Company's World's 50 Most Innovative Companies list. This recognition places Cresilon at No. 1 in the medical devices category and No. 46 overall globally. The news was announced in New York on March 25, 2026, showcasing the company's commitment to innovation and excellence in the industry.
